name: Hawaii Plantation Village
tagline: Walk through restored plantation-era homes and learn how Hawaii's multicultural identity was built.
hours: Mon–Sat 10am–2pm. Closed Sundays. Guided tours at 10am, 11am, 12pm, 1pm
bestTime: First tour at 10am is least crowded and cooler
talkStory: Best for ages 6 and up — younger kids may not engage with the history. The guided tour walks through homes representing eight different ethnic groups that worked the sugar plantations. Older kids interested in Hawaii history will find it fascinating. About 1-1.5 hours total. A good educational stop near the Waipahu area. The gift shop has unique local items. Pair with a drive through old Waipahu town for lunch.
